task_completion.description: OpenBCI GUI is launched and configured for a Synthetic session.
task_completion.visual_evidence: OpenBCI GUI window is visible with 'SYNTHETIC' selected in the system control panel or data source dropdown.

task_completion.description: The synthetic EEG data stream is active.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Start Data Stream' button has been clicked (changes to 'Stop Data Stream') and EEG waveforms are actively scrolling in the Time Series widget.

task_completion.description: The Marker Mode widget is opened to allow marker insertion.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A widget panel titled 'Marker Mode' is visible in the GUI layout, displaying buttons for different marker values (e.g., 1, 2, 3).

task_completion.description: A data recording session is initiated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Start Recording' button is clicked (changes to 'Stop Recording') or a recording timer/filename is actively displayed in the GUI interface.

task_completion.description: Event markers 1, 2, and 3 are inserted during the recording.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Vertical marker lines appear on the Time Series graph, the marker buttons (1, 2, 3) in the Marker Mode widget are seen being clicked, or terminal/console output confirms markers were sent.

task_completion.description: The recording is stopped and saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The recording is stopped in the GUI, and/or a file manager or terminal shows the newly created recording file in '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings/'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application to generate the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenBCI GUI interface being clicked and manipulated, rather than terminal commands or scripts bypassing the UI to generate a mock file.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually create or edit the recording file using a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, gedit) are open with mock CSV/EEG data containing manually typed markers.

integrity.description: The recording file appears to be genuinely generated by the OpenBCI GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file is located in the default OpenBCI Recordings folder and follows the standard OpenBCI naming convention (e.g., OpenBCI-GUI-date-time.txt or .csv), rather than a manually named dummy file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using OpenBCI GUI's SYNTHETIC mode. The default recording directory is /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings. The agent must insert markers 1, 2, and 3 (default shortcuts Z, X, C) using the Marker Mode widget. A valid recording should be at least 10 KB (approx. 1.2 seconds of data at 250 Hz) and markers must be temporally separated by at least 100 samples (0.4 seconds). Look for the Marker Mode widget in the GUI, active data streaming, vertical lines in the Time Series graph indicating marker insertion, and the final file saved in the Recordings directory.
